business.headline: Govt approves 2% dearness allowance hike for central govt employees, pensionersThe Union Cabinet approved a 2% hike in Dearness Allowance for government employees and pensioners, along with key decisions including a Rs 13,000 crore Sovereign Maritime Fund and extension of PMGSY till 2028.

On paper, compliant: How labour laws are quietly underminedIn the third episode in this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, this article examines how employers maintain compliance on paper while systematically evading legal obligations in practice.

Digital gold, ETFs or SGBs: Where should you invest this Akshaya Tritiya?Akshaya Tritiya is here, and gold buying is once again in focus. But instead of just buying jewellery, many are now exploring smarter ways to invest. So, which option, digital gold, ETFs or SGBs, makes the most sense?
Oil prices slide over 10% as Iran opens HormuzIran's foreign minister said all commercial ships can pass through the Strait of Hormuz during the remaining ceasefire period.

Your FY27 game plan: Simplifying insurance, investments, tax and emergency fundPart 3 of our 'MONEY MAKEOVER' series explains why the start of the financial year is the right time to focus on insurance, investments, tax and emergency fund planning to guide the rest of the year.

Explained: Why India slipped to 6th-largest economy despite strong growthIndia has slipped to the 6th-largest economy in the IMF's latest outlook. Why did it happen despite strong growth? IndiaToday.in spoke to economists to find out.
Sensex ends 500 points higher as HUL leads market rally with 5% riseThe S&P BSE Sensex added 504.86 points to end at 78,493.54, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 156.80 points to close at 24,353.55.

Sebi allows 50% IPO size cut without refiling: What it means for investorsThe regulator has taken this step as market participants are finding it difficult to mobilise resources and access capital markets in the current geopolitical environment.

Akshay Tritiya property plans? Don't miss these 5 key checksAkshay Tritiya is seen as the perfect day to make big financial moves, and property is high on many buyers' lists, but before you take the plunge, are you checking the right things?
DA hike delayed: Union body urges Govt to act quicklyThe timely announcement of DA will help restore confidence and ease concerns among lakhs of central government employees and pensioners.

Netflix shares slide over 9% as co-founder Reed Hastings steps downHastings has been a key figure in shaping Netflix into what it is today. He co-founded the company and played a major role in transforming it from a DVD-by-mail service into a global streaming giant.

Forced to mis-sell: IIIT graduate quits Rs 17 LPA bank job, cites sales pressureChirag Madaan's resignation from his Rs 17 lakh per annum banking job has reignited focus on mis-selling at financial institutions, an issue IndiaToday.in had earlier highlighted through its investigation into how sales targets and incentives shape what customers are sold.

Gautam Adani overtakes Mukesh Ambani to become Asia's richestThe latest ranking places Gautam Adani at the top spot in Asia, with Mukesh Ambani now in second place. Both remain among the top 25 richest people globally, even as their wealth levels have seen changes this year.

2% to 4% DA hike likely: Here's how much your monthly pay may riseA fresh DA revision is around the corner, and early estimates suggest a 2% to 4% hike, but how much extra could this actually add to your monthly salary? Let's have a look.

India slips to 6th-largest economy: What IMF's latest data showsIndia has slipped out of the world's top five economies in nominal GDP terms, going by the IMF's latest World Economic Outlook estimates. The change says more about currency than growth, with projections suggesting the loss in ranking may not last long.

Markets open flat as geopolitical tensions ease, but caution remainsThe BSE Sensex was trading at 78,030.99, up 42.31 points or 0.05% at around 9:43 am. The Nifty 50, however, slipped marginally by 6.85 points or 0.03% to 24,189.90.

Gurugram circle rates increase by up to 75%: How much will home prices rise?The revision in Gurugram circle rates comes at a time when the city continues to see strong demand due to its proximity to the airport and the presence of major offices and industries.

Rs 8 lakh crore stuck: How delayed payments are hurting India's MSMEsAcross India, the total value of such pending payments to micro, small and medium enterprises is estimated at Rs 8.1 lakh crore, according to industry estimates and government assessments.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock markets rise or fall today?GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 24,178 at 8:06 am, suggesting that the Nifty 50 may open close to its previous session's level of 24,196.75. This points to a muted start for the markets after recent volatility.

Gold is rising slowly: Should you expect a big move soon?Gold has been moving up, but quietly. There's no sudden surge, just a gradual rise. So the big question is—does this signal a strong rally ahead or more sideways movement?

entertainment.headline: Scorpions cancel India tour due to medical emergency, ticket refunds assured German rock band Scorpions have cancelled their Coming Home India tour due to unforeseen medical issues affecting the members, disappointing fans across major cities. The band expressed regret but assured ticket refunds.

Vijay Deverakonda announces his next film with Hi Nanna director Shouryuv Vijay Deverakonda has announced his next ambitious film, VDxShouryuv, collaborating with director Shouryuv. Shouryuv is best known for his hit Hi Nanna, with actors Nani and Mrunal Thakur.

Zayn Malik hospitalised ahead of UK show, says still recovering Zayn Malik's much-awaited UK concert has been postponed due to a sudden health issue. As the singer focuses on recovery, organisers are working on new dates and will share updates, including refund options, soon.

Akshay Kumar shares disappointing news for Hera Pheri 3 fans amid legal trouble With Hera Pheri 3 facing delays, Akshay Kumar has now shared a rather disappointing update on the film. Unresolved issues and legal complications have been stalling the project for quite some time now.

Bhooth Bangla box office Day 1: Akshay scores his best opener since Housefull 5 Bhooth Bangla marks the reunion of Akshay Kumar and Priyadarshan. While the horror-comedy has started on a steady note, it trails the genre's recent big openers and has received mixed reactions for its humour and scares.

Dhurandhar 2 box office Day 30: Film ties with Pushpa 2, to soon dethrone Baahubali 2 The box office race between Dhurandhar 2 and Pushpa: The Rule has tightened, with both films hovering around Rs 1,742 crore globally after a month. As Dhurandhar maintains a steady run across markets, it now faces fresh competition from Bhooth Bangla.

Ramayana, Toxic, Alpha and more: Indian cinema's global dreams and real challenges Indian films are getting bigger, the audience is expanding globally and the ambition for a worldwide hit is real. But ambition and infrastructure are not the same thing, and right now the gap between the two is hard to ignore.

Feel like the King of the world: SRK reacts as Academy shares Om Shanti Om speech Shah Rukh Khan revisited his iconic Om Shanti Om speech after The Academy shared a clip, winning fans with his witty response. Up next, the actor is lining up new projects, including a reported cameo in Raaka and his film King.

Ryan Gosling's Project Hail Mary won't stream soon, makers extend theatre run Project Hail Mary will remain a theatrical-exclusive for now, with no immediate OTT release, as the makers push for a big-screen, IMAX-led experience. Starring Ryan Gosling, the sci-fi hit released in Indian theatres on March 26.

Avengers Doomsday: Trailer breakdown, cast, major reveals from CinemaCon The Avengers: Doomsday trailer premiered at CinemaCon 2026. The upcoming film, merges X-Men into the (MCU) Marvel Cinematic Universe.

Pakistani Dhurandhar? Internet mocks Javed Sheikh's show Jahannum Ba'raasta Jannat Social media users mocked the Pakistani show Jahannum Ba'raasta Jannat over the portrayal of RAW and the Hindi accent. Many on social media feel it is a copy of Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar.

Samay Raina visits Botez sisters' Los Angeles home, roasts them with zero filter Samay Raina recently visited the popular Botez sisters' Los Angeles home, where he roasted them left, right and centre for keeping their house messy. The vlog was shared by the sisters on their popular YouTube channel BotezLive.

Vikram's next Chiyaan 63 announced on actor's 60th birthday, watch teaser Actor Vikram's upcoming film Chiyaan 63, directed by Anand Shankar, promises a gritty, rustic story with the actor in an unconventional role. The teaser hints at a dark, suspenseful narrative and showcases his striking transformation.

Kalyani Priyadarshan, Pranav Mohanlal to marry? Lokah actor's mother reacts Kalyani Priyadarshan and Pranav Mohanlal are once again at the centre of dating rumours, with the Lokah star's mother Lissy Priyadarshan dismissing all such talk.

Yash's Toxic gets 9-minute extended preview at CinemaCon 2026 The makers of the Kannada film Toxic unveiled an exclusive nine-minute preview at CinemaCon 2026, offering a deeper look into the film's world and emotional depth. This gangster drama, headlined by Yash, is set for a June 2026 release.

Prakash Raj's veiled dig at Vijay's political entry: Can't give actors the country Actor Prakash Raj criticised actors entering politics, terming it a 'cinema model', in what appeared to be a veiled dig at his Jana Nayagan co-star and TVK chief Vijay. Raj urged voters to choose between the Dravidian, slave, or cinema models ahead of the Tamil Nadu elections.

Jailer 2 BTS video leaked online, makers of Rajinikanth's film issue strict warning A leaked BTS clip from Jailer 2, starring Rajinikanth, has surfaced online, prompting a strong response from production house Sun Pictures. Last week, Vijay Jaya Nayagan was released online in HD print.

Hansika Motwani on divorce from Sohael Khaturiya: Better to get off the wrong train Actor Hansika Motwani has opened up about her divorce from Sohael Khaturiya. She shared that she is in a better emotional space and grateful for her family's support during this personal phase.

Dhurandhar revived Bollywood: Kangana praises film, Madhavan's Ajit Doval act Kangana Ranaut doubled down on her praise for Dhurandhar 2. She appreciated R Madhavan's performance in Dhurandhar: The Revenge, while also crediting the film with bringing audiences back to Hindi cinema.

general news.headline: Iran reimposes restrictions in Strait of Hormuz, accuses US of violating ceasefireIran has reimposed restrictions on the Strait of Hormuz, reversing its brief reopening and warning it will continue blocking transit as long as the US maintains its blockade on Iranian ports.

Black day for NDA, faced defeat for 1st time: Priyanka after women's quota bill failsOn linking delimitation with the women's quota bill, Priyanka Gandhi questioned how the Opposition could allow a government, which she said has no regard for institutions, to alter the number of Lok Sabha MPs on such a large scale in the name of providing reservations to women in Parliament and state assemblies.

Govt approves 2% dearness allowance hike for central govt employees, pensionersThe Union Cabinet approved a 2% hike in Dearness Allowance for government employees and pensioners, along with key decisions including a Rs 13,000 crore Sovereign Maritime Fund and extension of PMGSY till 2028.

Hormuz open or closed? U-turn by Indian ships as Iran army reimposes curbsHours after Iran announced that the Strait of Hormuz was 'completely open' for commercial vessels, earning praise from Trump, four Indian and two Greek vessels failed to cross the energy chokepoint.

When India's secret naval blockade forced Pakistan to surrenderHandcrafted ByOur Editors The world may be feeling the pinch of Iran's blockade of Strait of Hormuz, the key oil supply lifeline, amid its war with the US. But Pakistan got a taste of it decades ago when India exerted a covert blockade during the 1971 war. Here's how it unfolded.

7 lies in an hour: Iran calls Trump's claims false, threatens to close Hormuz againJust as Trump was speaking at an event in Arizona on Friday evening, Iran's Parliament Speaker Mohammad Ghalibaf posted on X, stating that continued blockade would mean the Strait of Hormuz would not remain open. He emphasised that passage through the strait would occur according to a "designated route" and require Iranian authorisation.

Husband of TCS Nashik's Nida Khan reveals her location, police find house lockedNida Khan, who is currently absconding after allegations of a sex scandal and forced conversion racket surfaced at a TCS-linked BPO in Nashik, has been on the run since the case came to light.

RCB vs DC live: Will Bengaluru give KL Rahul the birthday blues at Chinnaswamy?Bengaluru (RCB) vs Delhi (DC) live score, IPL 2026: Can KL Rahul deliver on his birthday for Delhi Capitals? He returns to his home town as the Capitals look to arrest an early-season slide. RCB are sitting pretty at the top of the table. Can the visitors challenge their dominance today?

With sweat of 6 months, Cuddalore villagers bring back Buckingham Canal to lifeA 3-km stretch of the Buckingham Canal was restored by in Tamil Nadu's Cuddalore district as 600 villagers joined hands with the administration to remove 750 kg of plastic waste that was choking it. From fishing to drainage and transportation, this is how the canal will help Pichavaram village grow now.

Conspiracy to save Nashik godman, parents of TCS case accused Nida Khan claimThe parents of TCS Nashik conversion case accused Nida Khan' have denied all allegations of forcible conversion, calling the case fake and politically motivated. They said she is currently at her in-laws' home in Bhiwandi.

Live: Congress celebrated denying women their rights, says Smriti IraniWomen's Quota Bill Live Updates: The Lok Sabha on Friday rejected a bill that sought to fast-track the implementation of women's reservation and carry out delimitation without conducting a fresh Census. Out of 528 members who participated in the vote, 298 backed the Bill while 230 opposed it. It fell short of the 352 votes the National Democratic Alliance (NDA) needed to secure the required two-thirds majority.

Man attacks neighbour with sword suspecting affair with wife, almost severs earPreliminary investigation suggested that Nitin suspected an illicit relationship between his wife and Abhishek, which had led to several disputes between them in the past.

KV schools are topping India's boards. So why are bureaucrats opting out?Handcrafted ByOur Editors Kendriya Vidyalayas (KVs) are still outperforming most schools in the country. But while the results remain intact, the constituency they were meant to serve is quietly shifting, and that may matter more.

Live: Hormuz to remain under Iran control if US denies free navigation, warns IRGCUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: US President Donald Trump said negotiations with Iran are progressing well, but officials in Iran insist the US must lift its blockade on Iranian ports for the Strait of Hormuz to remain open. Tehran has also rejected claims that it agreed to transfer enriched uranium abroad, calling it a firm red line. Meanwhile, the US Central Command reported that 21 vessels have complied with orders to turn back since the blockade began on April 13. Analysts highlight significant differences in how both sides describe the ongoing talks. In a sign of limited maritime movement, a passenger cruise ship recently crossed the strait for the first time since the conflict began. Separately, US naval authorities denied reports of food shortages aboard deployed warships in the region. Follow indiatoday.in for all live updates.

Prakash Raj's veiled dig at Vijay's political entry: Can't give actors the countryActor Prakash Raj criticised actors entering politics, terming it a 'cinema model', in what appeared to be a veiled dig at his Jana Nayagan co-star and TVK chief Vijay. Raj urged voters to choose between the Dravidian, slave, or cinema models ahead of the Tamil Nadu elections.

Govt drops mandatory Aadhaar app plan after pushback from Apple and SamsungIndia has dropped its plan to make the Aadhaar app mandatory on smartphones after pushback from Apple, Samsung and other manufacturers.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi for India debut? What history says about teen prodigiesVaibhav Sooryavanshi has been in the spotlight amid discussions around a potential India call-up for the tour of Ireland later this year. But at just 15, the bigger question remains: would a debut at this age be a bold, forward-looking move or a step taken too early in his development?
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

Hormuz traffic to follow Tehran-set route after coordination: IranThe remark was made in a live televised interview on Friday, April 17, by Esmaeil Baghaei, Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesperson, who said that such vessels must also coordinate fully with Iranian authorities.

Meta set to fire 16000 employees in coming months, 8000 layoffs on May 20Meta is gearing up for another big round of layoffs, with about 8,000 employees expected to be cut starting May 20 and more cuts likely later in the year. The move comes as the company shifts focus heavily towards AI and tries to run leaner after years of aggressive hiring.

Ramayana, Toxic, Alpha and more: Indian cinema's global dreams and real challengesIndian films are getting bigger, the audience is expanding globally and the ambition for a worldwide hit is real. But ambition and infrastructure are not the same thing, and right now the gap between the two is hard to ignore.

College by day, hustle by night: Gen Z is rewriting work with side hustlesCollege is no longer just about degrees and deadlines. Across campuses, students are stepping into the workforce early. From freelancing to content creation, they are building side hustles alongside incomes. But between ambition and burnout, the hustle comes at a cost.

Delhi breaks 14-year April rainfall record. Will it end the heat?Delhi recorded its highest April rainfall in 14 years at the Palam observatory. The sudden rain was caused by a western disturbance and heat convection before temperatures hit 40 degrees Celsius.

People paid Rs 8 crore to run, lift at Hyrox Bengaluru event that has India talkingAround 9,000 people gathered at the Bangalore International Exhibition Centre on the weekend to do workouts one could easily do in a park or gym. Each person paid Hyrox over Rs 9,000 for that. It has sparked a debate over whether Hyrox workouts are merely a status symbol or a shift in how India approaches fitness.

sports.headline: RCB vs DC live: Will Bengaluru give KL Rahul the birthday blues at Chinnaswamy? Bengaluru (RCB) vs Delhi (DC) live score, IPL 2026: Can KL Rahul deliver on his birthday for Delhi Capitals? He returns to his home town as the Capitals look to arrest an early-season slide. RCB are sitting pretty at the top of the table. Can the visitors challenge their dominance today?

A promise kept: Frank Lampard helps Coventry secure promotion to PL after 25 years Coventry City ended a 25-year absence from the Premier League by securing direct promotion with a dramatic late equaliser against Blackburn Rovers. The achievement marks a remarkable turnaround for the club after decades of financial struggles, lower-league football, and exile from their home stadium.

Mahi Mania in Hyderabad, CSK eye first away win against rejuvenated SRH IPL 2026, CSK vs SRH preview: Chennai Super Kings arrive in Hyderabad on the back of two consecutive wins. There is 'Dhoni mania' in the city, but it remains to be seen whether the Super Kings can halt the SunRisers, who are threatening to peak after a slow start to their season.

When Shreyas Iyer's blinder made Jonty Rhodes feel like grandfather of fielding IPL 2026: Jonty Rhodes praised Shreyas Iyer's stunning fielding effort, joking that the modern standard has left him feeling like the 'grandfather of fielding' in comparison to today's athletic brilliance.

Congratulations after a loss? The bizarre exchange that left Ajinkya Rahane moved IPL 2026, GT vs KKR: As Ajinkya Rahane's winless KKR crumbled again in Gujarat, a rare post-match moment of empathy offered a stark emotional contrast. Rahane thanked a journalist who congratulated him for his hard work.

Brendon McCullum to rejoin KKR? Pietersen makes wild prediction IPL 2026: Kevin Pietersen predicts Brendon McCullum will return as KKR coach next season as the team remains winless in IPL 2026. Kolkata sits at the bottom of the table after a 5-wicket loss to GT.

MI should pay Deepak Chahar and Shardul, ask them to go home: Ex-India captain IPL 2026: Former captain Kris Srikkanth urged the Mumbai Indians team management to drop Shardul Thakur and Deepak Chahar, criticising the medium pacers' performance in their loss to Punjab Kings on Thursday.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi for India debut? What history says about teen prodigies Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has been in the spotlight amid discussions around a potential India call-up for the tour of Ireland later this year. But at just 15, the bigger question remains: would a debut at this age be a bold, forward-looking move or a step taken too early in his development?

Embrace tough times, try to do best for the people of Kolkata: Ajinkya Rahane GT vs KKR, IPL 2026: Ajinkya Rahane urges KKR to stay positive despite a winless start to IPL 2026. Following a loss to Gujarat Titans, the veteran batter promises to keep fighting for the loyal fans of Kolkata.

IPL 2026: Sunrisers Hyderabad bring in Gerald Coetzee to replace David Payne Sunrisers Hyderabad have signed South African pacer Gerald Coetzee for Rs 2 Crore to replace the injured David Payne. Coetzee brings his high-pace and international experience to the SRH bowling attack.

IPL 2026 Play Of The Day: KKR's shocking brainfade leaves Green high and dry vs GT GT vs KKR, IPL 2026: On a day when Cameron Green finally showed form and was proving to be the difference, KKR batters decided to hit the self-destruction button, leaving their big-money buy without a chance to cash-in.

Break for Hardik Pandya? Mumbai Indians skipper keeps gasping amid dreadful runIPL 2026: Hardik Pandya hasn't had the best of times as captain with Mumbai Indians losing four matches on the trot and languishing at the bottom half of the points table.

In-form RCB aim to extend dominance as KL Rahul returns to ChinnaswamyKL Rahul returns to his backyard as the Delhi Capitals leader, ready to challenge a red-hot RCB at the M. Chinnaswamy Stadium. While Bengaluru dons iconic green jerseys for their sustainability mission, they must overcome the "Green Curse" to stop Rahul from spoiling the party at his home turf.

India Women falter in 1st T20I vs South Africa, sloppy show dents World Cup prepSAW vs INDW, 1st T20I: Harmanpreet Kaur's India slumped to a six-wicket defeat in the series opener on Friday at the Kingsmead in Durban as South Africa took a 1-0 lead in the five-match series.

Beard look, 3 wins: Shubman Gill's new style turns lucky charm for GTIPL 2026, GT vs KKR: Since Shubman Gill adopted his new look, Gujarat Titans have gone on a three-match winning streak. On Friday, GT defeated the Knight Riders by five wickets.

Why wasn't Cameron Green trusted with final over vs GT? Ajinkya Rahane respondsKKR skipper Ajinkya Rahane revealed why he didn't turn to Cameron Green for the final over against GT as they lost another game in the final over of the match. Green scored a brilliant 79 but didn't bowl a single delivery in the second innings.

Shubman Gill 86 headlines GT's nervy win as Kolkata remain winlessIPL, GT vs KKR: Gujarat Titans made it three wins on the trot after losing their first two games, riding on a composed 86 from Shubman Gill to chase down 181 against Kolkata Knight Riders, who continue to search for their first win of the season.

Felt bad for Virat Kohli: Influencer reacts after RCB star trolled for likeInstagram influencer LizLaz reacted after Virat Kohli liked and later unliked her post, saying she felt bad seeing the RCB star get trolled online. Fans made fun of Kohli for liking the picture of the German influencer despite not following her account.

Ban RR manager Bhinder: Lalit Modi criticises BCCI leniency over protocol breachLalit Modi has slammed the Board of Control for Cricket in India and the IPL governing council for not banning Rajasthan Royals team manager Romi Bhinder and just imposing a Rs 1 lakh fine on him for a PMOA protocol breach of using his phone in the dugout.

Smriti Mandhana reigns supreme, topples Rohit Sharma to become India's top T20I scorerSAW vs INDW, 1st T20I: Smriti Mandhana went past Rohit Sharma to become India's top T20I run-scorer during the series opener on Friday against South Africa at the Kingsmead in Durban.

Neeraj Chopra, Sumit Antil complain of harassment by Dronacharya awardee coachSumit Antil has accused Dronacharya awardee coach Naval Singh of mental harassment and verbal abuse in a complaint submitted to the Sports Authority of India (SAI), with the allegations backed by Olympic champion Neeraj Chopra.

Green light at last: KKR's costliest player smashes 79 to silence criticsCameron Green was finally able to release some of the price-tag pressure after scoring 79 off 55 balls to help KKR score 180 in the first innings after a top-order collapse. Green, however, lost all the momentum towards the end of the innings.

Drop Ajinkya Rahane: Fans urge KKR to take tough calls after golden duck vs GTIPL 2026: Ajinkya Rahane faced heavy criticism after a golden duck against Gujarat Titans, with fans calling for his removal as KKR captain amid the team's poor run. Despite being among the team's top scorers, his leadership and tactical decisions continue to come under scrutiny.

Will Pat Cummins play vs CSK? Vettori clarifies as SRH plan tactical shiftPat Cummins will link up with Sunrisers Hyderabad on April 17, a day before their clash against Chennai Super Kings, but is expected to be available only from April 25 for the Jaipur fixture against Rajasthan Royals.

DC head coach backs Auqib Nabi to come good for the franchise in IPL 2026IPL 2026: Delhi Capitals head coach Hemang Badani has backed pacer Auqib Nabi to bounce back after a modest debut, citing his temperament and consistency as key strengths. He also downplayed concerns over Delhi Capitals' inconsistent start, stressing the importance of addressing weaknesses over chasing momentum.

Pakistan captain to quit T20Is? Salman Agha prioritises ODI World Cup preparationPakistan captain Salman Ali Agha is considering taking a break from T20I cricket to focus on preparing for the ODI World Cup. The 32-year-old has been struggling for form in the shortest format of the game.

Virat Kohli to play as Impact Player against Delhi? Hazlewood provides key updateIPL 2026: Josh Hazlewood has opened up about the possibility of Virat Kohli being an impact sub once again for RCB when they face DC at the Chinnaswamy stadium on Saturday, April 18. Kohli donned the role of the impact sub for the first time in his career during the win over LSG.

Novak Djokovic out of Madrid Open due to injury, sparks French Open fearsNovak Djokovic has announced his withdrawal from the Madrid Open due to ongoing injury concerns, raising apprehensions about his participation in the upcoming French Open.

Who is Connor Esterhuizen? GT sign South African sensation as Banton replacementSouth African sensation Connor Esterhuizen has been signed as Gujarat Titans to be a replacement player for the injured Tom Banton. Esterhuizen impressed for South Africa during his international debut against New Zealand.

Max Verstappen reacts to race engineer Lambiase's McLaren switchMax Verstappen revealed that race engineer Gianpiero Lambiase sought his approval before accepting a move to McLaren in 2028, with the Dutchman encouraging him to take the opportunity. Lambiase's exit comes amid a difficult start to the season for Verstappen and growing speculation about his own future at Red Bull.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
